name: The White House
subject: A vote to fund our troops
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=159bde15d61b599eb382e740f7ae2c9c0be9af04c687229babc40339d4774d6c2f813aef4f392a8429015ebad3297ead8935f729828d005a

A vote to fund our troops

In the coming 36 hours, members of both houses of Congress will vote on an “omnibus” spending bill to keep our Federal government open. Omnibus bills cover a broad range of priorities, eliminating the need for many separate votes.

President Donald J. Trump supports the deal crafted by leaders on Capitol Hill and urges its swift passage. “The President had a discussion with Speaker Ryan and Leader McConnell, where they talked about their shared priorities secured in the omnibus spending bill,” Press Secretary Sarah Sanders said yesterday. “The President and the leaders discussed their support for the bill.”

This bill addresses many important needs, including:

- The largest pay raise for American troops in a decade
- More than 100 miles of new construction for the border wall
- Key priorities here at home, such as combatting the opioid crisis and rebuilding infrastructure

Go deeper:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d4a060c0cc3315c05aba4bdf9f41d6e912c5db31d021da5d0556c963093a20cb60ed20e8430b46dd1fe5f67df285f10c9e4c1a80915b1407
How President Trump is rebuilding American strength

Ending online sex trafficking

Yesterday, the U.S. Senate followed the House of Representatives in passing the Allow States and Victims to Fight Online Sex Trafficking Act, meaning the bill will soon come to President Trump’s desk for signing. The President applauds the bipartisan proposal, which passed yesterday with a 97-2 vote.

“This bipartisan piece of legislation takes an important step forward in fighting the despicable act of human trafficking,” Press Secretary Sanders said yesterday. “The legislation empowers Federal, State, and local prosecutors to hold websites accountable for supporting the sale of sex trafficking victims.”

This legislation builds on the Administration’s far-reaching work during its first year to end modern slavery in all its forms. Ivanka Trump in particular has been a leader in that fight, hosting a White House discussion last week to lend her support to the bill.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7703b06a71c74d7aec5929671bd415a16965e39b4614134ef4c2ff2773951c2e92627fda1ddfdc905b59cde0e58495ccb0b7a985f6963b24
Read the White House’s full statement on this effort to fight online sex trafficking.

President Trump meets with the Crown Prince

As the United States continues to face threats from Iran and other sponsors of terror in the Middle East, Saudi Arabia remains one of America’s staunchest allies. This week, President Trump and Vice President Mike Pence met with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman in Washington.

President Trump and the Crown Prince discussed joint efforts to finalize new commercial deals that would support more than 120,000 American jobs. The Crown Prince also thanked the President for American leadership in defeating ISIS and countering Iran’s destructive actions across the Middle East.

The Administration is committed to a strong relationship with Saudi Arabia for years to come. President Trump said he looks forward to the visit of Saudi King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud to the United States later this year.

Watch:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9fdd08a989c28e8e1850224c501e4213745921d6818b34e5427f93c3abdb4a2a44bac0d42459a32495214de8916142bbd4cdefe479bde02d
President Trump meets with the Crown Prince in the Oval Office

Photo of the Day

The White House Rose Garden | March 21, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump will sign a Presidential Memorandum targeting China’s economic aggression before participating in a panel discussion at the Generation Next Summit.

This afternoon, Vice President Pence will travel to Manchester, New Hampshire, to deliver keynote remarks at an America First Policies “Tax Cuts to Put America First” event.

This evening, the President and First Lady will host the Greek Independence Day Celebration.

subject: I am gravely concerned, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
The only way we're going to be able to hold the Trump administration accountable is by electing more Democrats.
---
Derek --

When I was press secretary for President Obama, my strategy was simple: I spoke directly with the president and didn't make a habit of lying to the American people.

You and I both know that's not how the Trump administration operates. Between the constant staff upheaval and drama, the rogue tweets, and overall failure to put the interests of the American people first, it's clear this administration is in utter chaos.

We can't control what happens in Trump's White House, but what we can do is turn our energy and attention towards electing Democrats determined to hold him accountable.

**I am asking you to be a part of that effort. Chip in $3 today to elect Democrats in all 50 states this year -- it's the only way we'll be able to cut through the noise and get our message directly to voters.**

**[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052251c/4067b0a/51b927526/7e4b1b26/3959887660/VEsEAA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/trump-accountability?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=25+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180322_je_nd&source=em_20180322_je_nd&refcode=em_20180322_je_nd")
[DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052251c/4067b0a/51b927526/7e4b1b27/3959887660/VEsEAw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/trump-accountability?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=26+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180322_je_nd&source=em_20180322_je_nd&refcode=em_20180322_je_nd")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052251c/4067b0a/51b927526/7e4b1b24/3959887660/VEsEAg/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/trump-accountability?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=27+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180322_je_nd&source=em_20180322_je_nd&refcode=em_20180322_je_nd")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052251c/4067b0a/51b927526/7e4b1b25/3959887660/VEsEDQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/trump-accountability?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=28+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180322_je_nd&source=em_20180322_je_nd&refcode=em_20180322_je_nd")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052251c/4067b0a/51b927526/7e4b1b22/3959887660/VEsEDA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/trump-accountability?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=29+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180322_je_nd&source=em_20180322_je_nd&refcode=em_20180322_je_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052251c/4067b0a/51b927526/7e4b1b23/3959887660/VEsFBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/trump-accountability?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=30+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180322_je_nd&source=em_20180322_je_nd&refcode=em_20180322_je_nd")
** When I stood behind the podium in the White House briefing room, I understood that I had a duty to President Obama -- and a duty to the American people.

The American people not only deserve a government that works on their behalf -- they also deserve one that does it with integrity and transparency.

I am deeply concerned that the Trump administration is doing lasting damage to the bond between the American people and their government -- and I can imagine you feel the same way.

November is our chance to do something about it. We have a real opportunity to create the blue wave everyone is talking about. But it will only happen if we invest in Democratic candidates running in tough elections and help them get their message directly to voters.

Thank you for your support. Your contribution will help Democrats win elections nationwide -- we can't do it without you.

**[Contribute $3 now to invest in Democrats who will stand up to Trump and his White House and hold them accountable.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052251c/4067b0a/51b927526/7e4b1b20/3959887660/VEsFBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/trump-accountability?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=31+-+Contribute++if+consinConsGroup28497++set&utm_campaign=em_20180322_je_nd&source=em_20180322_je_nd&refcode=em_20180322_je_nd")**

Josh

Josh Earnest
Former White House Press Secretary

Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052251c/4067b0a/51b927526/7e4b1b2c/3959887660/VEsFAA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=35+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180322_je_nd&source=em_20180322_je_nd&refcode=em_20180322_je_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

---

Team --

Eight years ago this week, Obamacare became the law of the land.

Now, insurance companies cannot deny coverage to people with pre-existing conditions and millions more Americans can get the care they need.

But Trump and the GOP have stepped up their attacks on Obamacare -- and have even caused premiums to increase for working families. That's why it's crucial that we mark this 8-year anniversary with an outpouring of grassroots support for Obamacare.

[**Sign now to stand with Senate Democrats to stop the GOP 's attacks on Obamacare >>**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954cbaa/2b07114c/96d0d5f5/731e92f1/105165657/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/protect-obamacare-now?source=PT-20180322-AddYourName_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

If we don't fight to defend President Obama's legacy, critical patient protections could be lost and millions of Americans could lose their access to affordable care.

We can't go back, team. Senate Democrats are fighting hard to protect the gains we made under Obamacare in the face of Republican attacks -- and they need your support.

**Add your name right now: Stand with Senate Democrats to defend Obamacare:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/defend-obamacare**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954cbaa/2b07114c/96d0d5f5/731e92f1/105165657/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/protect-obamacare-now?source=PT-20180322-AddYourName_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Thanks,

Team DSCC

**A note on contributions, team:**

When we say that we're counting on grassroots donors to protect Senate Democrats and defend Obamacare, we're not kidding. In fact, a full 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We don't have the Koch brothers in our corner -- we have you. And every gift you give the DSCC is a gift we use to make Republicans regret their Obamacare attacks and replace them with Democrats in November.

Team --
Last week, the Republicans on the House Intelligence Committee announced their plans to end their investigation into Russia's interference in the 2016 election.
Then, Donald Trump spent the weekend attacking Special Counsel Robert Mueller's investigation.
With Trump ramping up his rhetoric against Mueller, it's imperative that Congress protects the Special Counsel. **We all need to speak up, including you:[Please sign the petition urging Congress to protect Mueller's investigation into Russia's interference in the 2016 election.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf38/2a380d31/57deda3/f1e08b/1201091105/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20180322_ProtectMueller_act?source=e20180321_protectmueller1_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")**
We'll be crystal clear: inaction is not an option here. Trump has a track record of ousting those he feels threatened by, and his increased attacks on Mueller indicate that he's getting nervous.
**Our democracy is on the line, and we cannot afford for Republicans to stand idly by and leave this investigation unprotected.**
Here's the link to our official petition calling on Congressional Republicans to defend our democracy. Your voice is so important, so please sign it today:
[**https://hassan.bsd.net/Protect-the-Russia-Investigation**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf38/2a380d31/57deda3/f1e08b/1201091105/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20180322_ProtectMueller_act?source=e20180321_protectmueller1_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")

-- Team Maggie

body:
Who are the Koch brothers, what do they want and what are some actions we can take to stop them?
#
Sisters and Brothers -
**First, the good news**. On Tuesday, with your help, the progressive community won major victories in the Illinois Democratic primaries. As the Washington Post reported, "voters resoundingly rejected Chicago-style machine politics." Chuy Garcia, a strong progressive who worked with me during our presidential campaign, took on the Chicago Democratic machine and won a landslide victory in his primary election. He will, almost certainly, be the next congressman from the third district of Illinois.
Chuy also helped elect a number of younger progressive who we supported. Alma Anaya, 28, won her primary election for the Cook County Board of Supervisors. Beatriz Frausto-Sandoval, an immigration attorney, won her race for Cook County Circuit Court judge and Aaron Ortiz, a 26-year-old school counselor, won his race for the Illinois State House. Progressives also won a major victory when Joe Berrios, the chairman of the Cook County Democratic Party and current County Assessor, and one of the most powerful political figures in the state, lost his reelection bid to progressive candidate Fritz Kaegi.
Thank you all for bringing the political revolution to Illinois.
**Now, the bad news**. Late last month, the Koch Brothers and their network committed to spend up to $400 million on the 2018 election as a thank you for the Republican Party's tax cuts for the rich, and "to change the trajectory of this country."
Given this news, I thought it was an appropriate time to ask the question that the media and most politicians won't ask: Who are the Koch brothers, what do they want and what are some actions we can take to stop them?
As you may know, the Koch brothers are the extreme right-wing "libertarian" family. They are the third wealthiest family in the country - worth some $120 billion. They also, unbelievably, have more political power than either the Republican Party or the Democratic Party. As this country slides into oligarchy and, as a result of Citizens United, we see one family not only making huge campaign contributions, but establishing think tanks, appointing university chairs and creating a number of organizations in almost every sphere of American political life. Their goal: make the very rich richer at the expense of everyone else.
Not only did they help pass the Republican tax bill - which will provide them with about a billion dollars a year in tax breaks - they are also working aggressively to move toward the privatization of the Department of Veterans Affairs, Social Security, Medicare and public education. In fact, they want to repeal virtually every piece of major legislation passed since the 1930s designed to protect the elderly, the children, the sick, the poor and the environment.
Now, it's hard for me to understand how you could have almost $100 billion and feel the desperate need to lower your taxes, or to take from those who are struggling, so you can have even more. I would think that with that much money you just might be able to get by. But given the news of their projected campaign spending, it doesn't appear that they are satisfied. Their greed demands more, more and more. They never stop.
What the Koch brothers and their friends want is to spread their unique vision of "freedom" in America. They want to dismember government so as to give Americans the "freedom" to live in poverty working for $3 or $4 an hour without health care, without child care, without a pension, without the ability to afford to send their kids to college and without any hope that their children will have a higher standard of living than they do.
They also want to end - not cut - but end, Social Security, Medicare and Medicaid. One of the Koch brothers even once ran for president on a platform of "the eventual repeal of all taxation."
Now, this isn't personal for me. I don't know the Koch brothers, but I do know this: the Koch brothers and billionaires like them have bought up the private sector and are now well on their way to buying up the government. And I can assure you, sisters and brothers, they aren't spending that money with the interests of working families, women and seniors in mind - they are doing it to elect candidates who will make the rich richer and everyone else poorer.
Let's be clear: Money dominates everything that goes on in Congress. Wall Street, the pharmaceutical industry, the coal and oil companies, agribusiness and the rest of corporate America spend billions every year not just on campaign contributions, but also on lobbying. That is why we have a government that represents the 1 percent, and ignores the needs of almost everyone else.
Do you want to know why we pay the highest prices in the world for prescription drugs, and why there are no regulations preventing the drug companies from selling their products for any price they want? It might have something to do with the fact that the pharmaceutical industry is one of the most politically powerful industries in the country and spends endless amounts of money on lobbying and campaign contributions.
In Washington, you get what you pay for, and the result is that the desires of the rich and powerful are well-attended to. The pain of working families is ignored.
This is the rigged economy held in place by a corrupt system of campaign finance that we talked about on our campaign. And it is just as important to take it on today as it was in 2015 and 2016.
**If we are serious about creating jobs, gun safety, about climate change and the needs of our children and the elderly, we must be deadly serious about campaign finance reform. And Congress must know you are committed to this fight:**
**[Sign my petition to say you support a constitutional amendment to overturn Citizens United. Congress needs to know that the American people want representatives in office who oppose the outsized influence millionaires and billionaires have on our elections.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/koch_bros?source=em180322-t1-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/koch_bros?source=em180322-t1-full")**
The Koch brothers understand the importance of politics. Meanwhile, people who work for low wages, have no health insurance and live in inadequate housing don't see a connection between the reality of their lives and what government does or does not do.
Showing people that connection is a very big part of what a true progressive movement has to do, because it is impossible to bring about real social change in this country if people are not involved in the political process.
Our vision for American democracy should be a nation in which all people, regardless of their income, can participate in the political process, can run for office without begging for contributions from the wealthy and the powerful.
Our job is not to think small in this pivotal moment in American history.
We must pass a constitutional amendment to overturn Citizens United. We need legislation that requires wealthy individuals and corporations who make large campaign contributions to disclose where their money is going. And more importantly, we need to move towards the public funding of elections.
The need for real campaign finance reform is not a progressive issue. It is not a conservative issue. It is an American issue. It is an issue that should concern all Americans - regardless of their political point of view - who wish to preserve the essence of the longest-standing democracy in the world, a government that is supposed to represent all of the people and not just a handful of powerful special interests.
And that starts with overturning the disastrous Citizens United decision:
**[Sign my petition to say you support a constitutional amendment to overturn Citizens United.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/koch_bros?source=em180322-t1-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/koch_bros?source=em180322-t1-full")**
What we showed in our presidential campaign - in a way that can change politics in America forever - is that you can run a competitive national grassroots campaign without begging millionaires and billionaires for campaign contributions.
That is what we must demand of our elected leaders: a complete rejection of their influence on campaigns. And that starts with making your voice heard.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Why I'm running
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
[Like anyone in my position, there is one question I have to answer: Why are you running?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=5PSd51Vj3fF5k6PfMeLyMqY5n2JzMJCB)

And the answer I keep coming back to is that I have some unfinished business to settle.

When I finished my term as Governor in 2010, I had no intention of running for another office. 16 years of service at the state and local level was a real honor but there were other challenges out there for me. Then a Senate seat surprisingly opened up in 2011. And I asked myself - _do I have any unfinished business?_

And I did. Despite my efforts campaigning against it, voters passed an anti-LGBT amendment to the Virginia Constitution in 2006. That was a real disappointment. And I had been working with Virginia Indian tribes for years to get them the federal recognition they deserved. I had testified on their behalf and helped our bipartisan Congressional delegation advocate for them. But we kept falling short.

So when I declared for Senate in 2011, I wanted to help make progress in these areas beyond what I could do in local or state office. _And we have._

I signed on to a Senate amicus brief advocating marriage equality for all and celebrated when it became law of the land in 2013. And I stood on the floor of the Senate with my colleague Mark Warner in January of this year when our tribal recognition bill finally passed.

While there is still much work to do on both these issues, on an elemental level I have been able to see two things that were important to Virginians completed. [But as my first term closes out and I ask myself again if I have any unfinished business left in my political service, the answer is: yes, now there's even more.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=vmsvj71KMnW8jbsq2K8t0aY5n2JzMJCB)

I want to make meaningful progress to protect our kids and communities from gun violence. I want to finally do comprehensive reform of our immigration laws so that we can continue to attract talented people from around the globe. I want to make sure that we offer students affordable college but also meaningful technical and apprenticeship opportunities. I want to keep reducing unemployment among veterans and military spouses. I want to drive prescription drug costs down and give folks more options for health coverage. _And there 's so much more._

You have taught me an important lesson. If you want to help people and you take the time to listen to them, there will ALWAYS be unfinished business on your "To Do" list. I have more to do and I would be honored to have your support as I keep at the work.

_Tim_
---
| [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=MwijtpKMQq0vTaMwP5yfA6Y5n2JzMJCB)

subject: We did it!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Lipinski for Congress
body:

Peter,

I'm excited to announce that we've officially won our primary for Illinois' 3rd Congressional District!

I want to thank all of you, my grassroots supporters, for helping us get here. Illinois working families are resilient, and they deserve a congressman who won't stop fighting for them. Thanks to you, I'll be able to keep fighting for American manufacturing, fair trade deals, and equal pay for equal work.

This race has been tough. There was a lot of misinformation spread about us and what we stand for. This I want to be clear about: We stand for you.

I am also praying for and wishing the best to my opponent. She ran a spirited race.

After election night, I went right back to Washington, DC to continue representing you and all my constituents, and am back to holding President Trump accountable and fighting for the communities of the Southwest Side.

[**We still have one more battle to fight though: Our Republican opponent in November is Art Jones, a reprehensible neo-Nazi and Holocaust denier. If you can, will you click here to make a contribution to help us make sure he has no chance?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/Lg5qq_H3msMs6LW8SAx3BHKe9NJd_CeWeU9T6x-adRs=/MrMWIg6bE2CV8QqKE8i8oeJRJUqqGpPCWAV8ZZjY8ls=?refcode=DL_EM_TY_WL_180322_1&amount=10.00&recurring=auto&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=50023&city=Ankeny&state=IA&phone=&occupation=&employer=)

Thanks again for everything.

Dan

Andrew launched his campaign to be Florida's next Governor because he's not afraid to stand up and fight for the people and issues that matter most.

From fighting for Medicare for All, public schools where our kids can truly learn, and an economy that puts working people first, to taking down the gun lobby in court, Andrew has walked the walk on what matters to us.

It's clear that Andrew is running the most energized, authentic, and progressive campaign in Florida history -- and now you don't just have to take our word for it: **We 're proud to announce that this campaign has been endorsed by Our Revolution!**

body:
| **Stand with student activists before the March For Our Lives!**

Friend --

This Saturday, student activists from our own state will be leading the call for real gun reforms with the March For Our Lives.

The NRA and even some elected Florida Republicans have tried to derail this movement, but we know Saturday will prove Americans want an end to the gun epidemic NOW.

[**Parkland student voices are leading the movement for gun reform, and we need to stand by their side and denounce every attempt by far-right extremists to silence them. SIGN YOUR NAME to stand with student activists before the March For Our Lives!**](http://act.floridadems. "https://act.floridadems.org/page/s/fdp-stand-with-our-student-activists-?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=2+-++if+consaddrstateabbr++FL+Parkland+s+els&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180322_1_1&source=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180322_1_1&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180322_1_1")

This movement was started by Florida students. They are our future, and they are proving grassroots organizing can promote the change we need!

But the cold and callous remarks against them by those on the extreme-right show that **we have got to vote these Republicans out.**

They're not representing their constituents or America's values. They represent the gun lobby and only the gun lobby.

[**SIGN ON to join us in standing shoulder to shoulder with the students calling for action. Add your name to support the March For Our Lives before Saturday!**](http://act.floridadems. "https://act.floridadems.org/page/s/fdp-stand-with-our-student-activists-?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=3+-+SIGN+ON+to+join+us+in+standing+shoulder+&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180322_1_1&source=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180322_1_1&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180322_1_1")

Thanks for your support,

Florida Democrats

| | | Friend --

How many more gun tragedies must we face before things change? How many more parents have to lose a child? How many more communities will gun violence tear apart before Congress acts?

**The upcoming March For Our Lives -- organized by student activists -- is a call to action, and I'm proud of the young people fighting to get meaningful gun violence prevention measures passed.**

These students have changed the conversation around gun laws and inspired a nation.

[**I stand with all who are raising their voices for change. Will you join me and show your support for the students leading the movement to stop gun violence?**](http://nm.martinheinrich. "https://nm.martinheinrich.com/page/s/support-student-activists-today?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&city=Albuquerque&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=1+-+I+stand+with+all+who+are+raising+their+v&utm_campaign=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180322_1_1&source=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180322_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180322_1_1")

But we can act.

Add your name today and show your support for the students leading the movement to stop gun violence.**](http://nm.martinheinrich. name: Bob Casey
subject: the president wants Pennsylvania again
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bob Casey for Senate
body:
A lot has happened over the past month, and I wanted to make sure you were up to speed:
1. President Trump visited Moon Township, PA, where he promised my opponent that he'll "be out fighting for him very, very hard."

2. With the help of supporters like you, Conor Lamb narrowly won his special election in Southwestern Pennsylvania in spite of the over $14 million that Republicans spent trying to defeat him.

3. My campaign manager told me that our own rapid response fund, which we need to push back against ads just like the ones that Conor faced, is $4,100 short of where it needs to be.

This is the exact moment when we have to be fully prepared for an onslaught of Republican smears, so we need to build up that rapid response fund as soon as possible.

I'm grateful for all of the support you've already given me, and I want you to know that. Can you take the next step and become a campaign donor by chipping in $5 or whatever you can to help me close this budget gap?

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: My heated exchange with Secretary Carson
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | Peter, This morning, I had it out with Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) Ben Carson over his proposed cuts to the HUD budget and his spending habits. When I voted to confirm Secretary Carson, I had some hope he would do a decent job. It turns out I may have been wrong. Carson's policies would, among other things, charge renters making three, six, nine thousand dollars a year as much as $1,800 more for housing. In an age where tax cuts are already being handed to the richest one percent and increasing the deficit, I find these policies fairly remarkable -- **and entirely unacceptable.** HUD under Secretary Carson cares more about replacing a wobbly dining chair than improving unsafe, unsanitary, and unfair conditions in public housing. I keep fighting like hell on behalf of struggling Americans -- but I need your support to defend my seat from opponents who will blindly support this administration's destructive agenda.
